explanation: We interpret the requirement as: Whenever apnea is detected (atomic proposition 'apnea'), the system must switch from PSV mode to PCV mode (PCVMode), and it should use the pre-determined apnea backup settings for RR_AP, PinspAP, and I:E_AP. The values for RR_AP and PinspAP are set by the user, while I:E_AP is fixed at 1:2. Translating to LTL: 'If apnea is detected, then eventually PCVMode is active and settings for RR, Pinsp, and I:E are correct.' The available atomic propositions mapping these settings are: RR_equalTo_RR_AP (respiratory rate is set to RR_AP), P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP (inspiratory pressure set to PinspAP), ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP (I:E AP set), and PCVMode (system is in PCV mode). So, we combine these with 'and' and use the implication with 'apnea': (apnea -> F(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)). This implication is required globally, so we wrap it in G: G(apnea -> F(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)).

explanation:
The natural language requirement describes the following: 'If apnea is detected' - this is represented by the proposition 'apnea'. 'The system shall automatically switch from PSV to PCV mode' - this is a transition from 'PSVMode' to 'PCVMode', i.e., when apnea happens and the system is in PSVMode, eventually it should be in PCVMode. Also, additional assignments for backup settings (RRAP, PinspAP, I:E_AP) are suggested but only some of these can be expressed directly in LTL using the given atomic propositions. There are atomic propositions indicating that RR=RR_AP, P_insp=P_inspAP, ItoE=ItoE_AP, so we can specify that after apnea is detected, the system eventually sets these parameters accordingly.

Thus, the formalization is: Whenever apnea is detected, then eventually (F) all of the following must hold: system is in PCVMode (PCVMode), RR=RR_AP (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), P_insp=P_inspAP (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and ItoE=ItoE_AP (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). Since it is a system-wide requirement, we use the global operator G.

The full formula is: G(apnea -> F(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP))

explanation:
The natural language requirement is: "If apnea is detected, the system shall automatically switch from PSV to PCV mode with pre-determined apnea backup settings for RR AP , PinspAP , I:E AP . RRAP and PinspAP shall be set by the user. I:E AP will be fixed at 1:2". The atomic proposition for detecting apnea is 'apnea'. The action the system must take is to switch from Pressure Support Ventilation mode (PSV mode) to Pressure Controlled Ventilation mode (PCV mode) -- both are atomic propositions: 'PSVMode', 'PCVMode'. The requirement specifies that when apnea is detected, the ventilator shall enter PCV mode and use backup apnea settings. There are three backup settings (RRAP, PinspAP, I:E AP); we have the atomic propositions for two settings the user must set (RR_equalTo_RR_AP, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and a third that must be fixed (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). 

Thus, the temporal logic should capture: whenever (globally) apnea is detected, eventually the system will enter PCV mode with the backup settings�c(ie, RR=RR_AP, P_insp=P_inspAP, ItoE=ItoE_AP). Equivalently, for every moment that apnea holds, it is required that (in the future) all the backup settings are active simultaneously with PCV mode. That is: G(apnea -> F(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP))

explanation: The requirement describes that if apnea is detected, the system should switch from PSVMode (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode) to PCVMode (Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode), AND certain parameters (RR_AP, PinspAP, I:E AP) must be set to their apnea backup values. RRAP and PinspAP are set by the user, while I:E AP is fixed (and can be considered as always set correctly). In temporal logic, this is captured by: whenever apnea is detected (atomic proposition 'apnea'), then eventually the mode becomes PCVMode AND RR_equalTo_RR_AP AND P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP AND ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP. Since this switch should happen whenever apnea is detected, we need a global implication: G(apnea -> F(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)).

explanation:
The requirement states: If apnea is detected, then the system shall switch to PCV mode (from PSV), and set the appropriate parameters (RRAP and PinspAP set by the user, I:E AP fixed at 1:2).  In terms of atomic propositions, 'apnea is detected' is 'apnea', 'system in PCV mode' is 'PCVMode', and the parameter configurations can be expressed as 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'. (We assume the proposition ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP reflects the I:E AP is set to the fixed value of 1:2.) 'If apnea is detected, the system shall automatically switch...' is represented as an implication: 'apnea -> ...'. Moreover, this should hold globally throughout the system operation, so we use the global (G) operator at the top level. Thus, the LTL formula is:
G(apnea -> (PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP))
This formula says: at every moment, if apnea is detected, then (from that point) the system shall be in PCV mode, and the apnea backup settings (respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio) shall have the prescribed values. In reality, the 'shall automatically switch' requirement could be interpreted temporally (i.e., after apnea, eventually the system switches), but since no delay or allowed response time is specified, we interpret it as an instantaneous, hard requirement for this translation.

explanation_dict.natural_language: If apnea is detected
the system switches to PCV mode with apnea backup settings (RR, Pinsp set by user, I:E fixed)
If apnea is detected, system switches...
Always (at all times)

explanation:
The core requirement is: If apnea is detected, the system shall automatically switch from PSV to PCV mode with apnea backup settings for RR_AP, PinspAP (set by the user), and I:E_AP (fixed at 1:2). 

Since RRAP and PinspAP are set by the user, and I:E_AP is always fixed at 1:2, we'll focus on the mode switch and appropriate parameterization. The main property is: whenever apnea is detected (apnea), the system shall switch from PSVMode to PCVMode and ensure (eventually, within a reasonable time) that RR is set to RR_AP, P_insp is set to P_inspAP, and I:E is set to ItoE_AP (1:2). 

This leads to: G(apnea -> F(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP))

Step-by-step:
- 'If apnea is detected' maps to 'apnea'.
- 'System shall switch from PSV to PCV with settings...' becomes: 'eventually (after apnea) in PCVMode and RR=RR_AP and Pinsp=P_inspAP and ItoE=ItoE_AP'.
- The implication is global (holds always): whenever apnea is detected, this must happen.
- Each individual condition (mode & settings) is conjoined.
- We do not encode the fact that RRAP and PinspAP are user-set and ItoE_AP is fixed (the formula above requires only that the system sets to those values); further constraints are out of scope for basic LTL.

explanation: We begin by identifying the condition 'If apnea is detected', which translates to the atomic proposition 'apnea'. The requirement is that when apnea is detected, the system must automatically switch from PSV to PCV mode with pre-determined apnea backup settings for RR_AP, PinspAP, I:E_AP. The backup settings are in effect (RR equal to RR_AP, P_insp equal to P_inspAP, ItoE equal to ItoE_AP). Therefore, whenever apnea holds, globally it must be the case that these three settings hold until apnea is cleared (assuming settings should persist as long as apnea is true). We use implication (->), the global operator (G) to cover the requirement everywhere, and the until operator (U) if settings must persist while apnea holds. If settings must be set immediately after apnea is detected, we can use X (next) for switch, but typically in LTL such requirements are captured as: G(apnea -> (RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)). Settings for RR_AP and PinspAP are by the user, and ItoE_AP is fixed, but the setting actions are not temporal but configuration; thus we omit explicit user actions. The final formula is: G(apnea -> (RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)).

the system shall ... switch mode to PCV with settings as follows

explanation:
The sentence says: If apnea is detected, the system shall switch from PSV to PCV mode (automatically) with pre-determined apnea backup settings for three parameters (RR_AP, PinspAP, I:E_AP). Two (RR_AP and PinspAP) are set by the user; I:E_AP is fixed. 

We encode only the fragment related to automatic mode switch upon apnea detection. The action is: when apnea is detected (apnea), the system must eventually (F) be in PCV mode (PCVMode) and have set the relevant parameters. The measurable propositions in the atomic proposition list are:
- apnea (apnea is detected)
- PCVMode (system is in PCV mode)
- RR_equalTo_RR_AP (respiratory rate is set to apnea backup rate, as set by user)
- P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP (inspiratory pressure in PCV is set to the correct backup value, as set by user)
- ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP (I:E ratio is set to the predefined value for apnea backup)

The requirement is: Whenever apnea is detected, then eventually all these hold together (conjunction). This means globally (for every time point): if apnea -> eventually (PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP).

Globally (at every time point)

explanation: First, map the key events from the statement: 'If apnea is detected' is the atomic proposition 'apnea'. 'the system shall automatically switch from PSV to PCV mode with pre-determined apnea backup settings' can be modeled as 'apnea' implies eventually the system enters PCV mode with certain settings. 'PCVMode' is the atomic proposition for being in PCV mode. RRAP and PinspAP must be set by the user, so 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP' and 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP' respectively represent that the RR and inspiratory pressure are set according to the apnea backup (AP) profile. 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP' represents the I:E ratio fixed for apnea backup. These state settings must all hold when PCVMode is entered due to apnea. The logical structure is: If apnea holds, then eventually (in the future) all these should be true together (PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). This is most succinctly written as G(apnea -> F(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: apnea is detected
system switches from PSV to PCV mode with apnea backup settings when apnea is detected
whenever apnea is detected, system shall eventually be in PCV mode with the proper settings

explanation: The key requirement is: 'If apnea is detected, the system shall automatically switch from PSV to PCV mode with pre-determined apnea backup settings for RR AP , PinspAP , I:E AP . RRAP and PinspAP shall be set by the user. I:E AP will be fixed at 1:2.' In LTL, we can express the main requirement as: Whenever apnea occurs (apnea becomes true), eventually (F) the PCV mode must be entered, with the relevant backup settings applied. Since LTL only directly captures propositional logic, mode switches and parameter values must be represented as atomic propositions. The atomic propositions available include 'apnea', 'PCVMode', 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'. The commonly used pattern is: G(apnea -> F (PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)). This formula states "globally, whenever apnea is detected, eventually the mode is PCV and all backup apnea settings are applied." User controllability of RR and Pinsp, and fixity of I:E, are design requirements but not directly capturable in standard LTL unless we have atomic propositions for 'RRAP set by user' and similar (which are not present); so they are not included in the strict temporal logic here.
